definition: Lack of fairness or justice; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: What, to the American slave, is your 4th of July? I answer: a day that reveals to him, more than all other days in the year, the gross injustice and cruelty to which he is the constant victim.
source: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
speech: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?

definition: The practice of claiming standards to which one’s own behavior does not conform.
example: He named the hypocrisy of praising freedom while protecting the machinery of bondage.
source: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
speech: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?

definition: A formal accusation; a powerful public charge against wrong.
example: His Fourth of July oration was an indictment of a nation celebrating liberty while denying it.
source: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
speech: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?

definition: A forceful or violent effort to get free of restraint or resist attack
example: If there is no struggle, there is no progress. Those who profess to favor freedom, and yet depreciate agitation, are men who want crops without plowing up the ground.

definition: Forward or onward movement toward a destination; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: If there is no struggle, there is no progress. Those who profess to favor freedom, and yet depreciate agitation, are men who want crops without plowing up the ground.

definition: A state of anxiety or nervous excitement; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: If there is no struggle, there is no progress. Those who profess to favor freedom, and yet depreciate agitation, are men who want crops without plowing up the ground.

definition: The fact or power of enduring an unpleasant or difficult process or situation without giving way
example: The limits of tyrants are prescribed by the endurance of those whom they oppress.

definition: Keep (someone) in subjection and hardship, especially by the unjust exercise of authority
example: The limits of tyrants are prescribed by the endurance of those whom they oppress.

definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants
example: Freedom is the hinge of Lectures on literacy and freedom: without it the rest is decoration.
source: Various lectures on literacy and freedom

definition: Treat or regard (someone) with contempt or disrespect
example: The soul that is within me no man can degrade.
source: Speech at the American and Foreign Anti-Slavery Society

definition: A state of anxiety or nervous excitement; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Agitation is the hinge of What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?: without it the rest is decoration.
source: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
speech: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?

definition: A series of linked metal rings used for fastening or securing something
example: No man can put a chain about the ankle of his fellow man without at last finding the other end fastened about his own neck.
source: Speech in England / various American addresses

definition: Death, destruction, or some other terrible fate; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: The arm of the Lord is not shortened, and the doom of slavery is certain.

definition: Make (someone) troubled or nervous; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: The appeal was not to wait, but to agitate—West India Emancipation made delay look like surrender.

definition: The absence of sound or noise; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Silence is the hinge of What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?: without it the rest is decoration.
source: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
speech: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?

definition: The quality or state of being true; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: I expose slavery in this country, because to expose it is to kill it. Slavery is one of those monsters of darkness to whom the light of truth is death.
source: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
speech: What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?

definition: A hole dug in the ground to receive a coffin or corpse
example: I would rather be a free man in my grave than a slave beneath the sod.
source: Speech at the American Anti-Slavery Society

definition: The ability to read and write; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Literacy is the hinge of Lectures on literacy and freedom: without it the rest is decoration.
source: Various lectures on literacy and freedom

definition: The refusal to accept or comply with something; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Resistance is the hinge of West India Emancipation: without it the rest is decoration.
